factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with slight organic declines in some areas but overall net sales are increasing.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the quarter ended June 30, 2024 was due to an 8% increase from acquisitions, partially offset by a 2% organic sales decline.

factors.summary: Backlog orders decreased slightly compared to the previous quarter.

evidence.text: The Company's backlog of unfilled orders at June 30, 2024 was $3,403.0 million, a decrease of $131.1 million or 3.7% compared with $3,534.1 million at December 31, 2023.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2024 compared with the second quarter of 2023

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating as there is a 2% organic sales decline.

evidence.text: The increase in net sales for the second quarter of 2024 was due to an 8% increase from acquisitions, partially offset by a 2% organic sales decline.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2024 compared with the second quarter of 2023

factors.summary: The automation and engineered solutions core businesses are experiencing customer inventory normalization, which is affecting demand.

evidence.text: EMG experienced customer inventory normalization in our automation and engineered solutions core businesses, which we expect will continue through the remainder of 2024.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving, driven by higher sales and operational excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: Segment operating margins were negatively impacted in the second quarter of 2024 by the dilutive impact of the 2023 acquisitions. Excluding the dilutive impact of recent acquisitions, segment operating margins increased 190 basis points compared to the second quarter of 2023, due to the continued benefits from the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2024 compared with the second quarter of 2023

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction programs are contributing to margin improvements.

evidence.text: Excluding the dilutive impact of these acquisitions, segment operating margins increased 190 basis points compared to the second quarter of 2023, due to the continued benefits from the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2024 compared with the second quarter of 2023

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to operational excellence initiatives.

evidence.text: Excluding the dilutive impact of recent acquisitions, segment operating margins increased 190 basis points compared to the second quarter of 2023, due to the continued benefits from the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2024 compared with the second quarter of 2023

factors.summary: Supply chain health is stable with inventory normalization but still cautious due to customer inventory adjustments.

evidence.text: EMG experienced customer inventory normalization in our automation and engineered solutions core businesses, which we expect will continue through the remainder of 2024.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are normalizing, which could lead to risks of shortages or obsolescence.

evidence.text: EMG experienced customer inventory normalization in our automation and engineered solutions core businesses, which we expect will continue through the remainder of 2024.

factors.summary: Management is confident with their strategic initiatives and integration efforts.

evidence.text: The full year impact of the 2023 acquisitions, including the continued integration of Paragon, and focus on and implementation of our Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on our 2024 results.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include customer inventory normalization and integration challenges, which could impact demand and margins.

evidence.text: EMG experienced customer inventory normalization in our automation and engineered solutions core businesses, which we expect will continue through the remainder of 2024. The integration costs reduced net income by $22.2 million.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategy focused on acquisitions and operational excellence.

evidence.text: The full year impact of the 2023 acquisitions, including the continued integration of Paragon, and focus on and implementation of our Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on our 2024 results.

factors.summary: Successful integration of recent acquisitions and operational excellence initiatives are key catalysts for improved results.

evidence.text: Excluding the dilutive impact of recent acquisitions, segment operating margins increased 190 basis points compared to the second quarter of 2023, due to the continued benefits from the Company's Operational Excellence initiatives.
evidence.subsection: Results of operations for the second quarter of 2024 compared with the second quarter of 2023

factors.summary: The company is maintaining shareholder returns with increased dividends and a stock buyback program.

evidence.text: Effective February 9, 2024, the Company’s Board of Directors approved a 12% increase in the quarterly cash dividend on the Company’s common stock to $0.28 per common share from $0.25 per common share. The Company repurchased $7.6 million of its common stock for the first six months of 2024, compared with $6.5 million for the first six months of 2023.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving, showing a year-over-year increase.

evidence.text: Free cash flow (cash flow provided by operating activities less capital expenditures) was $742.6 million for the first six months of 2024, compared with $673.9 million for the first six months of 2023.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing to growth, though integration costs are affecting short-term results.

evidence.text: The integration costs reduced net income by $22.2 million. The full year impact of the 2023 acquisitions, including the continued integration of Paragon, and focus on and implementation of our Operational Excellence initiatives are expected to have a positive impact on our 2024 results.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage is improving, as evidenced by the decreasing net debt-to-capital ratios. The amended and restated credit agreements suggest strategic refinancing activities aimed at strengthening the balance sheet.

evidence.text: The debt-to-capital ratio was 22.3% at June 30, 2024, compared with 27.5% at December 31, 2023. The net debt-to-capital ratio (total debt, net less cash and cash equivalents divided by the sum of net debt and stockholders’ equity) was 19.6% at June 30, 2024, compared with 25.0% at December 31, 2023.
Exhibit 10.1 and 10.2 are Amended and Restated Credit Agreements dated May 12, 2022, and June 17, 2024, respectively.
